analysis_result: PTPRD mutation frequency in the benign/atypical NF oncoprint = 2/36 = 5.56% (approximately 6%). Two samples (Tumor_2477 and Tumor_2503, both Atypical_NF) harbor PTPRD non-silent (missense) mutations: p.N465S and p.G251R.
denominator_used: N = 36; benign/atypical NF samples with WES data, defined as clinical data (MOESM4) rows where Tumor Type is in {'Neurofibroma', 'Atypical_NF'} and WES == 'X' (15 Neurofibroma + 21 Atypical_NF).
assumptions_made: Non-silent mutations defined as any Variant_Classification except 'Silent' (i.e., Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del/Ins, Splice_Site, In_Frame_Del/Ins, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation); barcode matching done by prefixing 'Tumor_' to the 'WES label' column in MOESM4 to match 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode' in MOESM5; 4 of 36 benign NF samples (Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_882, Tumor_2414) have no rows in the MOESM5 mutation table (no passing somatic calls) but are retained in the n=36 denominator since the oncoprint includes all profiled samples.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM4 'Clinical Data' sheet: 108 rows x 15 cols (Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, WES, WES label, etc.)
   - MOESM5 'Supplementary Table 2': 7457 mutation rows x 123 cols (Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, HGVSp_Short, etc.); 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcodes.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: Tumor Type in {Neurofibroma, Atypical_NF} --> 35 + 21 = 56 rows
   - Filter 2: WES == 'X' --> 36 samples (15 Neurofibroma + 21 Atypical_NF)
   - Final analytic cohort: n = 36.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Sample barcode derived as 'Tumor_' + <WES label> (e.g., WES label 2477 --> Tumor_2477).
   - Non-silent = Variant_Classification in {Missense_Mutation (4572), Nonsense_Mutation (158), Frame_Shift_Del (120), Splice_Site (85), In_Frame_Del (43), In_Frame_Ins (39), Frame_Shift_Ins (37), Translation_Start_Site (7), Nonstop_Mutation (6)}; Silent (2390 rows) excluded.
   - 4 of 36 benign NF barcodes (Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_882, Tumor_2414) absent from MOESM5 (no somatic mutation calls passed filtering); these contribute 0 to numerator, retained in denominator.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Numerator: unique benign-NF Tumor_Sample_Barcodes with at least one Hugo_Symbol == 'PTPRD' and Variant_Classification != 'Silent'.
   - Denominator: 36 (all benign/atypical NF WES samples).
   - Result: 2 samples (Tumor_2477 p.N465S Missense; Tumor_2503 p.G251R Missense).

E. RESULT
   - PTPRD mutation frequency in benign NF oncoprint = 2/36 = 5.56% (~6%).

F. CAVEATS
   - 4 of 36 samples have no rows in the mutation table; assumed to have no PTPRD mutations (zero-mutation or filtered samples are standard in oncoprint denominators).
   - No silent PTPRD mutations were present, so the silent-vs-non-silent distinction is moot for PTPRD in this cohort.
   - Oncoprint figures typically round to the nearest whole percent; 5.56% would typically appear as '6%' on the oncoprint.
